title: tylophora augustiniana
intro: Tylophora augustiniana, also known as Madabaru or Kutu, is a type of tropical vine native to the forests of Sri Lanka and parts of India, belonging to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Tylophora augustiniana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and exhibit anticancer properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared as a decoction or extract, often used in the form of a tea or powder, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
